mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on future cash flows and raises equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing. The signal is confirmed if rate cuts are followed by positive forward returns and refuted if positive returns occur without easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines, or if triggered easing periods show negative average returns across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5%, policy becomes restrictive and equity multiples compress through higher financing costs and lower risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The pattern fails if rate-hike regimes do not coincide with weaker forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold 20d returns are positive more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20d return after FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE together with slower PAYEMS growth indicates deteriorating labor income and household demand, which weakens earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is confirmed only when both labor indicators worsen together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint UNRATE-up and PAYEMS-down episodes do not produce lower 60d returns than either series alone, or if the joint signal has no incremental explanatory power in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS reflects stronger nominal household spending and revenue momentum, supporting earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if retail sales growth does not precede equity strength.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth does not coincide with above-baseline 20d returns, or if the sign of the relationship turns negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO after a prior monthly increase in CPIAUCSL signals stagflationary supply pressure: weaker real activity combined with persistent inflation compresses margins and raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is activated only when inflation is already elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL do not underperform the unconditional 20d return distribution, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST leads GDP and broad demand by signaling stronger construction activity, credit demand, and household formation, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when housing starts are accelerating from depressed levels.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebounds are not followed by higher 60d returns relative to flat or declining HOUST periods, or if GDP-confirmed rebounds do not improve signal strength.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or falling unemployment with rising PAYEMS supports consumer income, reduces recession odds, and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The short-horizon effect should vanish when unemployment is rising or payroll gains are fading.
failure_condition: Reject if 5d forward returns are not higher when UNRATE is flat-to-down and PAYEMS is rising, or if the signal is indistinguishable from noise in any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad demand deceleration, implying weaker revenue growth and lower near-term equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if demand slows without any subsequent market weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales slowdowns do not lead to below-median 60d returns, or if the interaction loses sign consistency across successive rolling windows.
